reviewer: Codex independent revised track-08 lyric, prompt, and reference-role reviewer

evidence: The section sequence exactly matches the revised S08/C036 TrackSpec and locked fingerprint.
Both complete Verse-Pre-Chorus-Chorus cycles realize the E08 structure role before the Bridge and expanded final Chorus; the Coda is an original ending.
The E11 harmony role remains high-level and the E12 binding is limited to rain, separation, recurring return, and continuing care without source-specific imagery.
The output contains exactly three text fences with the title outside them.
Positive form, instrumentation, harmony, and mix direction stay in Basic; unusable traits stay in Exclusion; Lyrics contains only supported tags and English lyrics.
The 74 BPM pulse, pad-bass opening, clean guitar, delayed electric piano, support-only kick, and dark ambience form one coherent nocturnal ballad.
Hook prominence comes from title repetition and a stable low contour, not a higher melody, louder percussion, or denser layers.
No audible result is inferred; this is a written-design judgment.
Same Time Next Sunday is a five-syllable title hook and the concrete answer to the repeated visit question.
The final Chorus deepens the appointment into a commitment to remember for both people without pitch or loudness escalation.
The revised English is grammatical and performable, including I'll bring your cardigan; we'll finish the page.
Each vocal section consistently allows about two bars per phrase, and the five title syllables map naturally to the specified five-note hook.
This is a text-level prosody judgment only.
Every vocal section is fully populated, both nonvocal sections contain no lyrics, and all returns are written in full.
The 66-bar plan at 74 BPM calculates to 214.1 seconds.
Positive instrumentation names pad, bass, clean guitar, delayed electric piano, and support-only kick; piano intro, percussion-led output, violin, and cello stay in Exclusion.
The A3-C5 range and mostly sub-Bb4 melody agree with the >C5 and belting exclusions.
Track 8 keeps the rainy-night, low-register, narrow-dynamic playlist identity while centering a weekly intergenerational care promise.
Its cardigan repair, shared crossword, directly given lemon drops, remembered school lunches, and Sunday appointment are distinct from Tracks 1-7.
The cardigan repair and crossword create practical reasons to return, while the repeated question directly causes the Sunday answer and hook.
The directly given lemon drops connect past caregiving to the final reciprocal-care promise.
An older loved one can forget dates while retaining long-practiced care habits; the promise is specific and proportionate rather than an implausible cure.
The lobby, crossword, cardigan, lemon drops, rainy drive, car seat, home arrival, and keys belong to one grounded visit-and-return context.
The visit ends at nine, the speaker drives one rainy city mile, and arrives home at nine-thirty without an unexplained gap.
Before the seven o'clock news defines the next visit's arrival time; the nonvocal Coda introduces no later event.
I remains the visiting daughter and you remains the same older loved one throughout.
The final us joins the same two people in a shared memory commitment.
The crossword, cardigan repair, sleeve-catching question, direct lemon-drop gift, rainy departure, and home arrival form one causal sequence.
The school-lunch line explains the lemon drops, while If Sunday slips away from you is resolved literally by I'll remember it for us.
Setting the cardigan beside the keys follows logically from carrying it inside after parking.
